name: 1 Channel Zigbee Retrofit Relay

description.consumer:
Your switch. Smarter.

The Retrofit Relay fits quietly behind your existing wall switch and gives it a new intelligence — responding to your schedule, your voice, or your app. Nothing on your wall changes. Everything about how it works does.
description.technical:
A single-channel Zigbee switching relay designed to retrofit any standard mechanical switch into a smart switch — no rewiring, no hardware replacement.

Installs behind the existing wall switch in a flush box or on DIN rail. Supports direct load switching up to 4A; for loads between 4A and 10A, an external relay, SSR, or contactor is required. Communicates natively over Zigbee 3.0 with full Home Assistant support, and integrates with Matter and Apple HomeKit via a compatible hub.

Key Specifications
- Channels: 1
- Max Switching: 10A
- Direct Load: Up to 4A
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Compatible with: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)
- Installation: Flush box or DIN rail

⚠ For loads above 4A, an external relay, SSR, or contactor is required.

specs.summary: Voltage: 90–250V AC | Max Current: 10A | Wireless Protocol: Zigbee 3.0 | Operating Temperature: -10°C to +40°C | Max Case Temperature (Tc): +80°C | Wireless Range: ≤200m (open field) | Dimensions: 52 × 47 × 18 mm | IP Rating: IP20 | Installation Type: Behind Switch / DIN Rail
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)

limitations: For loads above 4A use external relay / SSR / contactor

name: 1 Channel Zigbee Retrofit Relay (40A)

description.consumer:
Heavy loads. Light control.

The 40A Relay brings your high-power appliances — water heaters, pumps, heavy equipment — into your smart home without compromise. Schedule them, automate them, monitor them. The same effortless control, built for serious loads.
description.technical:
A heavy-duty single-channel Zigbee relay rated for direct switching of high-current loads up to 40A. Designed for high-power appliances such as water heaters, pumps, HVAC loads, and industrial equipment.

Installs behind an existing wall switch in a flush box or on DIN rail. Proper cable sizing, circuit protection, and installation practices are mandatory for safe operation at rated current. Communicates natively over Zigbee 3.0 with Home Assistant, and integrates with Matter and Apple HomeKit via a compatible hub.

Key Specifications
- Channels: 1
- Max Switching: 40A
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Compatible with: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)
- Installation: Flush box or DIN rail

⚠ High-current installation. Proper cable sizing, circuit protection, and qualified installation are required.

specs.recommended_load: High-power appliances and heavy electrical loads
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)

limitations: Proper cable sizing and protection devices mandatory. Qualified installation required.

name: 2 Channel Zigbee Retrofit Relay

description.consumer:
Two switches. One module. Zero effort.

Control two circuits from a single module hidden behind your existing switches. Lights in two rooms, a fan and a light — whatever the combination, each channel responds independently. Your walls stay the same. Your home gets smarter.
description.technical:
A dual-channel Zigbee switching relay that converts two standard mechanical switches into smart switches simultaneously — without replacing any existing hardware.

Each channel operates independently with direct load switching up to 4A. For loads above 4A per channel, an external relay, SSR, or contactor is required. Installs behind existing switches in a flush box or on DIN rail. Communicates natively over Zigbee 3.0 with Home Assistant, and integrates with Matter and Apple HomeKit via a compatible hub.

Key Specifications
- Channels: 2 (independent)
- Max Switching: 5A per channel
- Direct Load: Up to 4A per channel
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Compatible with: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)
- Installation: Flush box or DIN rail

⚠ Direct load switching only — not a dry contact output and not a dimmer.

specs.summary: Voltage: 90–250V AC, 50/60Hz | Current: Total 10A | Wireless Protocol: Zigbee 3.0 | Operating Temperature: -10°C to +65°C | Case Temperature (Tc): +80°C Max | Operating Range: ≤70m | Dimensions: 40 × 40 × 18 mm | IP Rating: IP20 | Frequency Band: 2.405–2.480 GHz | Max Radio Transmit Power: <+10 dBm | Installation Type: Behind Switch / DIN Rail
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)

limitations: For loads above 4A per channel use external relay / SSR / contactor. Not a dimmer. Not dry contact.

name: 1 Channel Zigbee Retrofit Dimmer

description.consumer:
The right light. Always.

Bright for the morning. Warm for the evening. The Dimmer learns when you want a room to feel different — and adjusts without you asking. Fits behind your existing switch. Works with your existing lights.
description.technical:
A single-channel Zigbee phase-cut dimmer module designed to retrofit any standard wall switch with intelligent brightness control — no switch replacement required.

Supports trailing and leading edge phase-cut dimming up to 200W for compatible dimmable lighting fixtures and drivers. Installs behind the existing switch in a flush box or on DIN rail. Communicates natively over Zigbee 3.0 with Home Assistant, and integrates with Matter and Apple HomeKit via a compatible hub.

Key Specifications
- Channels: 1
- Max Load: 200W
- Dimming Type: Phase-Cut (Trailing/Leading edge)
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Compatible with: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)
- Installation: Flush box or DIN rail

⚠ For use with dimmable fixtures and drivers only. Not a 0-10V controller and cannot function as a plain on/off relay.

specs.summary: Voltage: 90–250V AC | Max Power: 100W × 2CH (220V) | Wireless Protocol: Zigbee 3.0 | Operating Temperature: 0°C to +40°C | Case Temperature (Tc): +80°C Max | Dimensions: 40 × 40 × 18 mm | IP Rating: IP20 | Frequency Band: 2.405–2.480 GHz | Max Radio Transmit Power: <+10 dBm | Installation Type: Behind Switch / DIN Rail
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)

limitations: Compatible with dimmable loads only. Not a 0-10V controller. Does not function as a simple on/off relay.
dependencies: Zigbee coordinator or hub. Dimmable fixtures or drivers required.

name: 2 Channel Zigbee Retrofit Dimmer

description.consumer:
Two circuits. One mood.

Set the scene across two lighting circuits from a single module. The 2-Channel Dimmer sits invisibly behind your existing switches, independently managing the brightness of each circuit — responding to schedules, scenes, or a quiet tap.
description.technical:
A dual-channel Zigbee phase-cut dimmer that retrofits two standard wall switches with independent brightness control — without replacing any existing hardware.

Each channel independently supports phase-cut dimming up to 100W for compatible dimmable lighting fixtures and drivers. Installs behind existing switches in a flush box or on DIN rail. Communicates natively over Zigbee 3.0 with Home Assistant, and integrates with Matter and Apple HomeKit via a compatible hub.

Key Specifications
- Channels: 2 (independent)
- Max Load: 100W per channel
- Dimming Type: Phase-Cut (Trailing/Leading edge)
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Compatible with: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)
- Installation: Flush box or DIN rail

⚠ For use with dimmable fixtures and drivers only. Not a 0-10V controller and cannot function as a plain on/off relay.

specs.summary: Voltage: 90–250V AC | Max Power: 75W (110V) / 150W (220V) | Wireless Protocol: Zigbee 3.0 | Operating Temperature: 0°C to +40°C | Case Temperature (Tc): +80°C Max | Dimensions: 40 × 40 × 18 mm | IP Rating: IP20 | Frequency Band: 2.405–2.480 GHz | Max Radio Transmit Power: <+10 dBm | Installation Type: Behind Switch / DIN Rail
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)

limitations: Compatible with dimmable loads only. Not a 0-10V controller. Does not function as a simple on/off relay.
dependencies: Zigbee coordinator or hub. Dimmable fixtures or drivers required.

name: Zigbee Shutter Module

description.consumer:
Your blinds open with the morning. They close before the afternoon heat sets in. You didn't touch them.

The Shutter Controller brings your motorized curtains, roller blinds, and shutters into your smart home — responding to schedules, conditions, or a single tap. One device, quietly managing the light and privacy of your space. Fits behind your current wall switch. Disappears into your home.
description.technical:
A single-channel Zigbee shutter controller designed for AC motorized curtains, roller blinds, and shutters. Provides interlocked directional outputs — Up, Down, and Stop — eliminating any possibility of simultaneous conflicting signals to the motor.

Installs behind the existing wall switch in a flush box or on DIN rail. Communicates natively over Zigbee 3.0 with full Home Assistant support, and integrates with Matter and Apple HomeKit via a compatible hub.

Key Specifications
- Channels: 1 motor channel
- Control Type: Interlocked directional (Up / Down / Stop)
- Max Load: 2A
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Compatible with: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)
- Installation: Flush box or DIN rail

⚠ Designed exclusively for AC motor loads. Not suitable for DC motors or use as a lighting relay.

specs.summary: Voltage: 90–250V AC, 50/60Hz | Current: 2A | Max Power: 500W (250V) | Wireless Protocol: Zigbee 3.0 | Operating Temperature: -10°C to +40°C | Case Temperature (Tc): +80°C Max | Operating Range: ≤200m | Dimensions: 52 × 47 × 18 mm | IP Rating: IP20 | Installation Type: Behind Switch / DIN Rail
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)

limitations: AC motor loads only. Not for DC motors. Must not be used as a lighting relay.

name: Analog 0-10V Zigbee Dimmer

description.consumer:
Professional lighting. Smart control.

The 0-10V Dimmer bridges commercial-grade LED lighting systems with your smart home — giving you smooth, precise brightness control through the same interface as every other device. Simple to automate. Invisible once installed.
description.technical:
A single-channel Zigbee 0-10V analog dimming controller designed to integrate with LED drivers and lighting systems that accept a 0-10V dimming input signal.

Outputs a low-voltage 0-10V control signal — it does not switch or dim mains voltage directly. Designed for DIN rail or panel installation. Communicates natively over Zigbee 3.0 with Home Assistant, and integrates with Matter and Apple HomeKit via a compatible hub.

Key Specifications
- Channels: 1
- Output: 0-10V analog control signal
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Compatible with: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)
- Installation: DIN rail / electrical panel

⚠ Control signal output only — does not switch or dim high-voltage loads directly. Compatible 0-10V LED drivers required.

specs.summary: Input Voltage: AC100–240V 50Hz/60Hz | Output Voltage: 0–10V DC Max. 20mA | Dimming Type: 0–10V | Operation Frequency: 2.412GHz–2.484GHz | Operation Temperature: -10°C to +40°C | Protocol: Zigbee 3.0 | Dimensions (W × D × H): 39 × 39 × 18 mm | IP Rating: IP20
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant (native), Matter & Apple HomeKit (via hub)

limitations: Does not switch or dim high-voltage loads directly. Control signal output only.
dependencies: Zigbee coordinator or hub. Compatible 0-10V LED drivers required.

name: Zigbee Gateway (Wired)

description.consumer:
The hub your home thinks through.

Everything connects to this. The Zigbee Gateway sits quietly on your network — wired for stability — and gives your entire smart home a reliable brain. Pair it once. Trust it always.
description.technical:
A wired Zigbee 3.0 gateway that acts as the central coordinator for all Zigbee devices in the system. Connects via Ethernet for a stable, low-latency network backbone.

Supports up to 128 Zigbee devices and enables device pairing, scene automation, remote control, and unified device management through compatible mobile applications and automation platforms.

Key Specifications
- Device Type: Zigbee Coordinator / Gateway
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Network: Wired Ethernet (LAN)
- Capacity: Up to 128 Zigbee devices
- Installation: Indoor, fixed LAN connection

⚠ Does not control electrical loads. A stable wired Ethernet connection is required for reliable operation.

specs.summary: Wireless Connection: Zigbee | Max Connected Devices: 300 (wired) | Working Temperature: -10°C to +55°C | Power Input: USB-C 5V 2A | Connectivity: RJ45 Ethernet | Working Humidity: 10%–90% RH (non-condensing)
specs.compatibility: Home Assistant and compatible Zigbee automation platforms

limitations: Does not control electrical loads. Requires stable LAN connection for operation.

name: Zigbee Gateway (Matter)

description.consumer:
One home. Every ecosystem.

Zigbee devices. Matter devices. Apple HomeKit. The Matter Gateway connects them all — no friction, no switching between apps. Your home speaks one language, even when your devices don't.
description.technical:
A wired smart home gateway that bridges Zigbee 3.0 and Matter protocols, enabling unified control across both ecosystems through a single stable Ethernet connection.

Supports up to 128 devices and provides device pairing, cross-protocol bridging, scene automation, and remote control. Enables integration with Matter-enabled applications and Apple HomeKit via Matter.

Key Specifications
- Protocols: Zigbee 3.0 + Matter (IP / Thread bridge)
- Network: Wired Ethernet (LAN)
- Capacity: Up to 128 devices
- Compatibility: Matter apps, Apple HomeKit (via Matter)
- Installation: Indoor, fixed LAN connection

⚠ Does not control electrical loads. Acts as a communication and bridging layer only.

specs.summary: Dimensions: 90 × 90 × 22 mm | Power Input: 5V 1A | Power Interface: USB Type-C | Wireless Protocol: Zigbee, Thread | Operating Temperature: -10°C to +55°C | Operating Humidity: 10%–90% RH (non-condensing) | Storage Temperature: -20°C to +60°C | Storage Humidity: 5%–90% RH (non-condensing)
specs.compatibility: Matter-enabled apps, Apple HomeKit (via Matter), supported automation platforms

limitations: Does not control electrical loads. Cross-protocol bridging requires Matter-compatible ecosystem.

name: Convergia

description.consumer:
Your home. Fully understood.

Convergia doesn't automate your home — it understands it. Every light, every climate system, every scene runs through a single platform that thinks locally, responds instantly, and never depends on a cloud server to keep your life running.
description.technical:
Convergia is a locally hosted smart automation gateway that unifies KNX wired systems and wireless ecosystems — Zigbee, Wi-Fi, Matter, and LAN/IP — into a single interoperable platform.

Built on an Intel NUC-based appliance, it runs all automation logic locally, ensuring reliability independent of cloud connectivity. Advanced rule-based automation supports complex triggers, conditions, and multi-step logic. Integrates AV systems and IP cameras directly into automation workflows.

Key Specifications
- Architecture: On-premise (locally hosted)
- Protocols: KNX, Zigbee, Wi-Fi, Matter, LAN/IP
- AV Integration: Yes
- Camera Integration: IP cameras
- Automation: Rule-based, local execution
- Interface: Web dashboard + mobile access
- Hardware: Intel NUC-based pre-configured appliance

⚠ Does not switch electrical loads. Functions as the central intelligence layer for the entire automation system. Qualified integrator installation recommended.

specs.compatibility: KNX, Zigbee, Wi-Fi, Matter, LAN/IP, AV systems, IP cameras

limitations: Does not switch electrical loads. Central intelligence layer only.

name: IR Blaster (WiFi)

description.consumer:
Every remote. One app.

Your AC, TV, and set-top box — all the devices that came with their own remotes — now respond to . No rewiring. Just point, configure, and let your home handle the rest.
description.technical:
A Wi-Fi IR blaster that sends infrared commands to legacy IR-controlled devices — TVs, air conditioners, set-top boxes, and other appliances — through your smart home network.

Connects to your local 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi network and transmits configurable IR codes via mobile apps and automation platforms. Requires line-of-sight to target devices.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Wi-Fi 2.4 GHz
- Control: Infrared (IR)
- Installation: Indoor, line-of-sight placement

⚠ IR line-of-sight to target devices is required. Does not switch electrical loads. RF devices not supported — see IR+RF Blaster.

specs.summary: Power Input: 5V DC 1A (Micro USB) | Connection: Wi-Fi 2.4GHz 802.11b/g/n | IR Control Distance: ≤8m | Working Temperature: 0°C to +50°C | Working Humidity: ≤85% RH | Dimensions: 68 × 68 × 19.5 mm
specs.compatibility: Compatible smart home apps and automation platforms

limitations: IR line-of-sight required. Does not switch electrical loads. RF devices not supported.

name: IR+RF Blaster (WiFi)
device_type: Wi-Fi IR + RF Blaster / Universal Remote Controller

description.consumer:
IR and RF. Nothing left out.

Some devices use IR. Some use RF. The IR+RF Blaster covers both — motorized curtains, fans, projectors, air conditioners, and more — unified in a single device and a single app.
description.technical:
A Wi-Fi IR and RF blaster that transmits both infrared and radio frequency commands to legacy remote-controlled devices — TVs, air conditioners, projectors, RF motorized curtains, fans, and RF switches.

Connects to your local 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi network and sends programmable IR and RF codes via mobile apps and automation platforms. IR requires line-of-sight; RF does not.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Wi-Fi 2.4 GHz
- Control: IR + RF
- Installation: Indoor, within signal range

⚠ Does not switch electrical loads. IR requires line-of-sight to target devices; RF does not.

specs.summary: Power Input: DC 5V/1A (Micro USB 2.0) | Standby Power: ≤0.5W | IR Control Distance: ≤8m (horizontal), ≤15m (opposite direction, environment dependent) | IR Control Direction: Multi-directional | RF Frequency: 433/315 MHz | Carrier Frequency: 38 kHz | Connection: Wi-Fi 2.4GHz 802.11b/g/n | Operating Temperature: 0°C to +50°C | Operating Humidity: ≤85% RH | Dimensions: 68 × 68 × 27 mm
specs.installation: Indoor, within signal range of target devices
specs.compatibility: Compatible smart home apps and automation platforms

limitations: Does not switch electrical loads. IR requires line-of-sight; RF does not.

name: IR Blaster w/ Analog Screen, Temp & Humidity Sensor (WiFi)
device_type: Wi-Fi IR Blaster with Environmental Sensor

description.consumer:
It senses. It responds. You don't have to.

When the room gets warm, the air conditioner turns on. You didn't schedule it — read the room. The IR Blaster with sensors watches your environment and acts on it, quietly and precisely.
description.technical:
A Wi-Fi IR blaster with integrated temperature and humidity sensors and an analog-style display. Transmits IR commands to legacy devices while simultaneously reporting real-time environmental data for automation logic.

Connects to your 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi network. Sensor data can be used as automation triggers — for example, activating an air conditioner when temperature exceeds a threshold.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Wi-Fi 2.4 GHz
- Control: Infrared (IR)
- Sensors: Temperature + Humidity
- Display: Analog-style (temp/humidity)
- Installation: Indoor, line-of-sight placement

⚠ IR line-of-sight to target devices is required. Does not switch electrical loads.

specs.summary: Power Input: DC 5V/1A (Micro USB, no adapter included) | Infrared Frequency: 38 kHz | IR Control Distance: ≤10m | LED Indicator: Blue | Connection: Wi-Fi 2.4GHz | Temperature Measurement Range: 0°C to +60°C | Temperature Accuracy: ±1°C | Humidity Measurement Range: 0%–99% RH | Humidity Accuracy: ±5% RH | Dimensions: 65 × 65 × 17 mm
specs.compatibility: Compatible smart home apps and automation platforms

limitations: IR line-of-sight required. Does not switch electrical loads.

name: Zigbee VRV Gateway

description.consumer:
Your entire HVAC system. One tap.

Commercial-grade air conditioning, brought into your smart home. The VRV Gateway connects your VRV/VRF system to — so every unit, every zone, responds to your preferences without requiring a separate app or remote.
description.technical:
A dedicated gateway that integrates VRV/VRF HVAC systems into a Zigbee-based smart automation ecosystem. Connects to the VRV/VRF outdoor unit via LAN cable and communicates wirelessly with a Zigbee gateway.

Enables remote control, real-time monitoring, and automation of the entire VRV/VRF air conditioning system through the Zigbee automation platform — without modifying the HVAC hardware.

Key Specifications
- Device Type: VRV/VRF to Zigbee Integration Gateway
- HVAC Connection: Wired LAN to outdoor unit
- Wireless: Zigbee 3.0 (to Zigbee gateway)
- Installation: Indoor, wired to outdoor unit

⚠ Communication bridge only — does not switch electrical loads. VRV/VRF system compatibility must be confirmed before installation.

specs.summary: Communication Protocol: Zigbee | AC Interface: X, Y (VRF communication port) | Additional Ports: RS485 Peripheral (×2), KNS, RS485 | Power: External power adapter (do not connect 220V directly) | Compatible Brands: Daikin, Samsung, TCL, Gree, Toshiba, Panasonic, Hitachi, Hisense, York, Mitsubishi Electric, Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, AUX, LG, Trane, Midea, Carrier, Bosch, Haier, McQuay and more
specs.installation: Indoor, wired LAN connection to outdoor unit

limitations: Communication bridge only. Does not switch electrical loads. VRV/VRF compatibility must be verified.
dependencies: Zigbee coordinator or hub. Compatible VRV/VRF system required.

name: Zigbee DALI Gateway

description.consumer:
Architectural lighting. Smart precision.

DALI lighting systems were built for professional environments — precise, flexible, professional-grade. The DALI Gateway brings that precision into your smart ecosystem, giving you scene control, tunable white, and RGBW automation through the same interface as everything else.
description.technical:
A Zigbee to DALI protocol gateway that integrates DALI lighting systems into a Zigbee-based smart automation ecosystem. Supports up to 64 DALI nodes with DT8 compatibility for tunable white and RGBW control.

Translates Zigbee commands into DALI control signals for switching, dimming, grouping, scene control, and tunable white/RGBW operation. DIN rail or panel-mounted installation.

Key Specifications
- Wireless: Zigbee 3.0
- Interface: DALI Bus (2-wire)
- Capacity: Up to 64 DALI nodes
- DALI Type: DT8 (tunable white / RGBW)
- Installation: DIN rail / panel

⚠ Does not power lighting loads directly. Communicates with DALI drivers over the bus. Existing DALI installation required.

specs.summary: Input Voltage: 24V DC | DALI Channels: 1 | Built-in DALI PS Output Voltage: 12–22.5V DC | Built-in DALI PS Output Current: 250mA max | DALI Current Consumption (PS disabled): Max. 2mA | Max DALI Devices: 64 | Supported Device Types: DT6, DT8 Tc, DT8 XY, DT8 RGBWAF | Connectivity: Ethernet (RJ45) | Control Modes: Broadcast, group, scene, timer & schedule | Built-in Battery & RTC: Yes | Installation: DIN Rail | Dimensions (L × W × H): 110 × 53 × 65 mm

limitations: Does not power lighting loads directly. Communicates with DALI drivers over the bus only.
dependencies: Zigbee coordinator or hub. Existing DALI bus installation.

name: LED Controller (CV-RGB+CCT)

description.consumer:
Every colour. Every white. One controller.

From vibrant RGB to tunable warm-to-cool white — the CV RGB+CCT Controller gives your LED strips full range. Set the mood for every moment, every room, automatically.
description.technical:
A constant voltage LED controller for RGB+CCT LED strips, providing independent control over color (RGB), white temperature (CCT), and brightness across 5 output channels.

Requires an appropriate constant voltage DC power supply (12V or 24V) matched to the LED strip specification. Designed for panel or concealed installation.

Key Specifications
- Output: CV (Constant Voltage)
- LED Support: RGB + CCT (5-channel)
- Channels: 5 (R, G, B, CW, WW)
- Installation: Indoor panel or concealed

⚠ Requires an appropriate CV DC power supply (12V/24V). Does not power LED strips directly from mains voltage.

limitations: Requires appropriate CV DC power supply. Does not power LEDs from mains directly.
dependencies: Compatible CV DC power supply (12V or 24V) required

name: LED Controller (CV-DIMMER)

description.consumer:
Simple. Smooth. Smart.

Clean, continuous dimming for single-color LED strips — from full brightness to a gentle glow. The CV Dimmer handles the detail so your lighting always feels exactly right.
description.technical:
A constant voltage LED dimmer controller for single-color low-voltage LED strips, providing smooth 0-100% brightness control through a compatible smart ecosystem.

Requires an appropriate constant voltage DC power supply (12V or 24V) matched to the LED strip specification. Designed for panel or concealed installation.

Key Specifications
- Output: CV (Constant Voltage)
- LED Support: Single-color
- Channels: 1
- Control: 0-100% dimming
- Installation: Indoor panel or concealed

⚠ Requires an appropriate CV DC power supply (12V/24V). Does not power LED strips directly from mains voltage.

limitations: Requires appropriate CV DC power supply. Single-color only. Does not power LEDs from mains directly.
dependencies: Compatible CV DC power supply (12V or 24V) required

name: LED Controller (CC-CCT)

description.consumer:
Warm in the evening. Crisp in the morning.

Tunable white lighting that adjusts to the time of day — and to you. The CC-CCT Controller manages both warmth and brightness automatically, making every room feel right without you thinking about it.
description.technical:
A constant current LED controller for CCT (tunable white) lighting fixtures, providing independent control of brightness and colour temperature across warm white and cool white channels.

Designed specifically for constant current LED loads. Not compatible with constant voltage LED strips. Suitable for panel or fixture-integrated installation.

Key Specifications
- Output: CC (Constant Current)
- LED Support: CCT (WW + CW)
- Channels: 2 (WW + CW)
- Control: Brightness + colour temperature
- Installation: Indoor panel or fixture-integrated

⚠ For constant current LED loads only. Not compatible with constant voltage LED strips.

limitations: Not compatible with constant voltage LED strips. Constant current loads only.
dependencies: Constant current LED loads matched to controller rated output current

name: Zigbee Contact Sensor

description.consumer:
Open. Closed. Your home knows.

When a door opens, the lights turn on. When a window is left open, you get a notification. The Contact Sensor is small, invisible, and always paying attention — so you don't have to.
description.technical:
A Zigbee 3.0 magnetic contact sensor that detects and reports the open/closed state of doors, windows, and other entry points to a Zigbee gateway or compatible hub.

Battery-powered with surface mount installation via adhesive or screws. Used for security monitoring and as an automation trigger.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Detection: Magnetic open/close
- Power: Battery
- Mounting: Surface mount (adhesive or screws)

⚠ Status sensor only — does not control electrical loads.

specs.summary: Wireless Connection: Zigbee | Battery: CR2032 (replaceable) | Low Battery Alarm: Yes | Operating Temperature: -10°C to +50°C | Dimensions (Host): 40 × 26 × 13 mm | Dimensions (Magnet): 26 × 9.5 × 11 mm
specs.compatibility: Compatible Zigbee gateways and automation platforms

limitations: Does not control electrical loads. Status feedback only.

name: Zigbee Motion Sensor + LUX Sensor

description.consumer:
The lights come on when you enter. Not when the sun is still up.

The Motion + LUX Sensor reads both movement and light — so automations happen only when they should. It's the difference between a smart home that reacts and one that actually understands.
description.technical:
A Zigbee 3.0 combined motion and ambient light sensor that reports both occupancy and LUX readings to a Zigbee gateway or compatible hub for automation and control logic.

Enables intelligent automations based on both presence and light conditions — for example, activating lights only when motion is detected and ambient light is below a set threshold. Battery-powered with wall or ceiling mount.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Detection: PIR motion + ambient light (LUX)
- Power: Battery
- Mounting: Wall or ceiling

⚠ Does not control electrical loads. Provides sensor data for automation triggers.

specs.summary: Input Voltage: 3V | Battery: CR2450 (replaceable) | Low Battery Warning: Yes | Communication Protocol: Zigbee (IEEE 802.15.4) | Operating Frequency: 2.405–2.480 GHz | Max RF Power: +10 dBm | Data Transmission Rate: 250 Kbps | Antenna: PCB antenna, gain 1.08 dBi | Detection Range: 8m | Detection Angle: 120° | LED Indicator: Single white lamp | Operating Temperature: -10°C to +50°C | Dimensions: ø52 × 46 mm (±1mm)
specs.compatibility: Compatible Zigbee gateways and automation platforms

limitations: Does not control electrical loads. Provides motion and LUX data for automation logic.

name: Zigbee Vibration Sensor

description.consumer:
If something moves, you'll know.

A knock on the safe. A window forced open. A cabinet disturbed. The Vibration Sensor catches what cameras miss — and tells you instantly, before anything else happens.
description.technical:
A Zigbee 3.0 vibration sensor that detects movement, vibration, and tampering on doors, windows, safes, cabinets, and other surfaces, reporting events to a Zigbee gateway or compatible hub.

Used for security monitoring, tamper alerts, and automation triggers. Battery-powered with surface mount installation.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Detection: Vibration and motion
- Power: Battery
- Mounting: Surface mount (adhesive or screws)

⚠ Event notification only — does not control electrical loads.

specs.summary: Working Voltage: DC 3V | Battery: 1.5V AAA × 2 | Standby Current: ≤10 µA | Alarm Current: ≤20 mA | Communication Protocol: Zigbee | Transmission Distance: 10–30m (indoor, unobstructed) | Data Update Frequency: Every 30 minutes | LED Indicator: Yes | Installation: 3M adhesive tape or screw | Working Temperature: -10°C to +50°C | Working Humidity: ≤95% RH (non-condensing)
specs.compatibility: Compatible Zigbee gateways and automation platforms

limitations: Does not control electrical loads. Event notification only.

name: Zigbee mmWave Sensor (USB)

description.consumer:
It knows you're there. Even when you're still.

Traditional motion sensors miss you the moment you stop moving. The mmWave Sensor doesn't. It detects presence — not just movement — so your lights stay on, your climate stays comfortable, and your home keeps responding.
description.technical:
A USB-powered Zigbee mmWave presence sensor that detects fine motion and human presence using radar technology — without relying on traditional PIR sensing.

Enables accurate presence-based automations such as lighting control and HVAC triggers even when minimal movement occurs. USB power allows flexible placement where mains wiring is unavailable.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Detection: mmWave radar (motion + presence)
- Power: USB
- Mounting: Flexible placement

⚠ Does not control electrical loads. Provides presence/motion data for automation triggers.

specs.summary: Power Supply: 5V 1A (USB) | Power Consumption: 0.5W | Communication Protocol: Zigbee | Radar Frequency: 24GHz–24.25GHz | Light Sensitivity Range: 0–10000 Lux | Operating Temperature: -10°C to +40°C | Operating Humidity: 0%–95% RH (non-condensing)
specs.compatibility: Compatible Zigbee gateways and automation platforms

limitations: Does not control electrical loads. Provides presence/motion signals for automation.
dependencies: USB power adapter or USB power source. Zigbee coordinator or hub.

name: Zigbee mmWave Sensor (Ceiling Mounted)

description.consumer:
A room that knows when it's occupied.

The Ceiling mmWave Sensor watches the whole room — not a narrow cone. It detects presence, not just motion, so every automation responds to whether someone is actually there. Not whether they waved.
description.technical:
A ceiling-mounted, mains-powered Zigbee mmWave presence sensor designed for accurate occupancy detection across a full room. Detects micro-movements for continuous presence sensing — not just initial motion.

Ideal for lighting control, HVAC triggers, and occupancy-based automation in residential and commercial spaces. Ceiling mount provides optimal coverage.

Key Specifications
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Detection: mmWave radar (motion + presence)
- Power: Mains powered
- Mounting: Ceiling mount

⚠ Does not control electrical loads. Provides presence/motion data for automation triggers.

specs.summary: Wireless Connection: Zigbee | Working Voltage: AC 100–250V | Working Current: ≤70mA | Working Temperature: -20°C to +70°C | Detection Distance: 0.3–9m (default 6m) | Sensitivity: 0–9 (default 7) | Target Detection Confirmation Time: 0–10s | Target Disappearance Delay Time: 0.5s–25 min (min. 15s recommended) | Light Sensing Range: 0–2000 Lux | Installation: Ceiling Mounted
specs.compatibility: Compatible Zigbee gateways and automation platforms

limitations: Does not control electrical loads. Provides presence/motion signals for automation.
dependencies: Mains wiring at ceiling mount point. Zigbee coordinator or hub.

name: TOQ 4T

description.consumer:
Four touches. Your whole room.

The TOQ 4T is a panel that disappears into your wall and lets your home respond at a touch. Four buttons. Any scene. Any load. Finished in glass, in the colour that suits your space.
description.technical:
A 4-button Zigbee capacitive touch panel for load switching, scene triggering, and dimming control in smart automation systems.

Each button operates independently and is configurable for toggle (bistable) or momentary (monostable) operation. Supports brightness control via compatible retrofit dimmer modules. Available in white or black glass with silver, gold, or black bezel options and configurable backlight colour.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 4 capacitive touch
- Relay: 10A per channel
- Direct Load: Up to 4A per channel
- Dimming: Via external retrofit dimmer
- Finish: White/Black glass + Silver/Gold/Black bezel
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ For loads above 4A, use an external SSR or contactor for safe operation and longer relay life.

limitations: For loads above 4A use external SSR or contactor.

name: TOQ 4T+1S
device_type: Zigbee Touch Control Panel with Integrated Socket

description.consumer:
Control your room. Charge your devices. One panel.

The TOQ 4T+1S combines smart control with everyday utility — four touch buttons for your home automation, a power socket, and USB charging, all in a single elegant wall panel.
description.technical:
A 4-button Zigbee capacitive touch panel with an integrated 6A power socket and USB-C/USB-A charging ports, combining smart load control with utility power access in a single wall panel.

Each button is independently configurable for toggle or momentary operation, supporting load switching, scene triggering, and dimmer control. Available in white or black glass with silver, gold, or black bezel options.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 4 capacitive touch
- Socket: 1 integrated (6A)
- USB: 1x USB-C + 1x USB-A
- Relay: 10A per channel
- Direct Load: Up to 4A per channel
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ For loads above 4A use an external SSR or contactor. Socket rated at 6A maximum.

limitations: For loads above 4A use external SSR or contactor. Socket rated 6A.

Eight circuits. One surface.

A full room — or an entire floor — controlled from a single panel. The TOQ 8T gives you eight independent touch points, finished in glass, responding to the lightest touch.

Each button is individually configurable for toggle or momentary operation. Supports dimming via compatible retrofit dimmer modules. Available in white or black glass with silver, gold, or black bezel and configurable backlight colour.

- Direct Load: Up to 4A per channel

Ten touches. Every light, every scene.

The TOQ 10T is for rooms and homes that demand complete control from a single point. Ten independent buttons. Finished in glass. Nothing out of reach.

A 10-button Zigbee capacitive touch panel for comprehensive load switching and scene control across up to 10 independent channels in residential and commercial installations.

Each button independently controls a load or triggers a scene. Configurable for toggle or momentary operation per button. Available in white or black glass with silver, gold, or black bezel and configurable backlight.

- Direct Load: Up to 4A per channel

device_type: Zigbee Touch Control Panel with Fan Control

description.consumer:
Lights and fans. One elegant panel.

Eight touch points for your lighting and scenes, plus a dedicated fan control — all in one glass panel. The TOQ 8T+1F handles the full room without compromise.
description.technical:
An 8-button Zigbee touch panel with a dedicated fan control channel for ceiling fans up to 120W, combining multi-circuit load control with integrated fan management in a single wall panel.

Each of the 8 buttons is independently configurable for toggle or momentary operation. The fan channel operates separately for speed and on/off control. Available in white or black glass with silver, gold, or black bezel.

- Fan Channels: 1 (up to 120W)
- Relay: 10A per button channel
- Direct Load: Up to 4A per button channel
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ For loads above 4A on button channels, use an external SSR or contactor.

name: TAC 1

description.consumer:
One touch. The right response.

The TAC 1 is a single button with complete intention. Load control or scene trigger — available in glass or polypropylene, in the finish that suits your space.
description.technical:
A single-button Zigbee smart switch for direct load control or scene triggering. Features a backlit button with customizable icon and is available in polypropylene (PP) or anti-glare glass (AG) in black, white, or gold.

Dimming can be achieved via compatible retrofit dimmer modules. Operates as a direct load switch or scene trigger within the Zigbee ecosystem.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 1
- Relay: 10A
- Direct Load: Up to 4A
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Materials: PP or AG glass
- Colours: Black, White, Gold
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ For loads above 4A, use an external SSR or contactor.

Two circuits. One switch.

The TAC 2 controls two loads from a single elegant switch face — light and fan, two rooms, or two scenes. Available in glass or polypropylene in the finish you prefer.

A 2-button Zigbee smart switch with independent load control or scene triggering per button. Backlit with customizable icons. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

- Direct Load: Up to 4A per load
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0

description.consumer:
Four circuits. Seamless control.

The TAC 4 gives you four independent control points on a single switch face — for rooms where every circuit matters and every detail is considered.

A 4-button Zigbee smart switch with independent load control or scene triggering per button. Backlit with customizable icons. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

description.consumer:
Six scenes. One wall.

The TAC 6 is a scene controller — not a switch. Press once and an entire room responds. Morning scene, evening scene, away, welcome. Six possibilities on a single, elegant surface.
description.technical:
A 6-button Zigbee scene control switch for triggering scenes and automations within a Zigbee ecosystem. Contains no relays and does not switch electrical loads directly.

Each button is independently configurable for scene or automation triggers. Backlit with customizable icons. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 6
- Function: Scene / automation triggers only
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Materials: PP or AG glass
- Colours: Black, White, Gold
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ No relay — does not switch electrical loads. Scene control only.

specs.summary: Input Voltage: 110–240V AC 50/60Hz | Communication Protocol: Zigbee | Product Size: 86 × 86 × 10.5 mm | Bottom Shell Depth: 28mm | Installation: 86 mounting box (depth >50mm) | Working Temperature: -10°C to +60°C | Working Humidity: ≤95% RH (non-condensing)

limitations: No relays. Does not switch electrical loads directly. Scene and automation control only.

name: TAC HL

description.consumer:
Your geyser. On your schedule.

The TAC HL puts your heavy appliances — water heater, pump, high-power loads — on a smart switch rated for the job. Set schedules, automate by presence, or control remotely. Same simplicity, for serious loads.
description.technical:
A single-button Zigbee smart switch rated for heavy electrical loads up to 20A — designed for direct control of geysers, water heaters, pumps, and other high-power appliances.

Backlit with customizable icon. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 1
- Relay: 20A
- Load: Geysers, heaters, pumps
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Materials: PP or AG glass
- Colours: Black, White, Gold
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ Heavy load application. Proper cable sizing, circuit protection, and qualified installation are required.

The TAC SH gives your motorized blinds and curtains a dedicated, physical control — three buttons, completely intuitive, available in the finish that suits your space.

A 3-button Zigbee shutter and curtain control switch with dedicated Open, Close, and Stop functions for AC motorized blinds and curtains up to 150W.

Backlit with customizable icons. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 3 (Open / Close / Stop)
- Motor: AC curtain/blind, up to 150W
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Materials: PP or AG glass
- Colours: Black, White, Gold
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ AC motor loads only. Not suitable for use as a standard lighting switch.

device_type: Zigbee Smart Switch with Scene Control

description.consumer:
Load control and scenes. One switch.

The TAC 6+ combines the practical with the experiential — four buttons that control circuits, two that set the scene. Everything your room needs, on a single surface.
description.technical:
A 6-button Zigbee smart switch with 4 load control buttons and 2 dedicated scene trigger buttons. Combines direct load switching with scene control on a single switch face.

The 4 load buttons share a 10A relay capacity; the 2 scene buttons contain no relay and trigger automations only. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 4 load + 2 scene
- Relay: 10A total (4 load buttons, shared)
- Direct Load: Up to 2A per load
- Scene Buttons: Trigger only (no relay)
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Materials: PP or AG glass
- Colours: Black, White, Gold

⚠ 10A total shared relay capacity across load buttons. For loads above 2A use external SSR or contactor.

limitations: 10A total shared across load buttons. Scene buttons have no relay. For loads above 2A use external SSR. Materials: PP or AG glass. Colours: Black, White, Gold.

description.consumer:
Any wall. Any location. No wiring.

The TAC SS goes anywhere — no electrician required. Stick it by the bed, at the entrance, on a kitchen island. Six scenes at your fingertips, wherever it makes sense.

A battery-powered Zigbee scene switch with 6 configurable buttons for wireless scene and automation triggering — no wiring required.

Can be wall-mounted or surface-mounted at any location without electrical installation. Backlit with customizable icons. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

A socket that fits the system.

description.consumer:
Your fan. Fully in control.

Speed, on, off — from the wall or from anywhere. The TAC F gives your ceiling fan the same smart control as every other device in your home.

A Zigbee smart fan control panel for ceiling fans up to 150W. Provides on/off and speed control with a dedicated fan control interface.

Integrates with the Zigbee automation ecosystem. Available in PP or anti-glare glass in black, white, or gold.

name: TAC K

description.consumer:
Turn to dim. Push to switch. That's it.

The TAC K makes lighting control physical again — but smarter. A single rotary dial that adjusts brightness, shifts colour temperature, and switches between rooms or groups. No screen. No menu. Just touch.
description.technical:
A Zigbee rotary smart dial for intuitive control of lighting brightness, colour temperature, and device groups. Supports up to 3 individual lights or 3 lighting groups via rotate and push interaction.

Rotate to adjust brightness or colour temperature. Push to switch between groups or trigger actions. Designed for wall-mounted installation.

Key Specifications
- Interface: Rotary + push
- Control: Brightness, colour temperature, group switching
- Scope: Up to 3 lights or groups
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ Does not switch electrical loads directly. Smart control interface only.

specs.channels: Up to 3 lights or 3 lighting groups

limitations: Does not switch electrical loads directly. Control interface only.

name: 4" Touch Control Panel

description.consumer:
Your whole home. Four inches of glass.

Touch a scene. Ask Alexa. Check a device. The 4" panel is a control centre that mounts on your wall and brings your entire home into one place — simple, immediate, and always responsive.
description.technical:
A 4-inch wall-mounted smart home touch panel with an integrated Zigbee gateway, Wi-Fi connectivity, and built-in Alexa voice control. Provides centralized control of smart home devices, scenes, and automations.

Features automatic screen brightness adjustment and supports remote app-based control alongside local touchscreen interaction.

Key Specifications
- Display: 4-inch touchscreen
- OS: Embedded Linux
- Wireless: Wi-Fi + Zigbee (built-in gateway)
- Voice: Built-in Alexa
- Functions: Scenes, devices, automations, status
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ Does not switch electrical loads. Control interface and Zigbee coordinator only.

specs.summary: Input: 100–240V AC, 50/60Hz | Rated Current: 74 mA | Wireless Protocol: Wi-Fi, Zigbee | Working Temperature: 0°C to 40°C | Working Humidity: 5%–90% RH (non-condensing) | Size (without base): 86.0 × 86.0 × 10.5 mm | Size (with base): 86.0 × 86.0 × 36.2 mm

limitations: Does not switch electrical loads. Control interface and Zigbee gateway only.

name: 4 Gang Scene Switch

description.consumer:
Four buttons. Twelve possibilities. Zero wiring.

Place it anywhere. Stick it to a wall, put it on a nightstand, keep it in a pocket. The 4 Gang Scene Switch gives you up to 12 scenes from four buttons — no installation, no electrician, no limits on where it goes.
description.technical:
A battery-powered Zigbee scene switch with 4 mechanical buttons, each supporting single press, double press, and long press actions — enabling up to 12 configurable scenes or automation triggers without wiring.

CR2430 battery with approximately 2-year life. Slim profile (~1.3 cm). Wireless installation via adhesive mount or portable use.

Key Specifications
- Buttons: 4 mechanical
- Actions: Single / double / long press per button (12 scenes total)
- Power: CR2430 (~2 years)
- Profile: ~1.3 cm slim
- Protocol: Zigbee 3.0
- Installation: Wireless, adhesive or portable

⚠ No relay — does not switch loads. Scene and automation triggers only.

specs.summary: Input Power: CR2430 button battery | Communication: Zigbee | Dimensions: 86 × 86 × 13 mm | Standby Power: 10 µA | Working Temperature: -10°C to +45°C | Working Humidity: <90% RH | Button Lifecycle: 50,000 presses | Frequency Band: 2400–2484 MHz | Max Radio Transmit Power: <+19 dBm
specs.compatibility: Compatible Zigbee gateways and automation platforms

limitations: No relay. Does not switch electrical loads. Scene triggers only. Battery: CR2430 (~2 years).
dependencies: Zigbee coordinator or hub. CR2430 battery.

name: 10" Touch Control Panel

description.consumer:
Your home has a face.

The 10" panel is the centrepiece of a smart home — a full control surface that manages every light, every scene, and every device, while also serving as your front door intercom. Proximity-aware. Always on. Always ready.
description.technical:
A 10-inch wall-mounted smart home control panel with integrated Zigbee and Bluetooth gateways, supporting 128+ devices per protocol. Features a built-in HD camera, doorbell intercom, proximity wake sensor, and customizable control interface.

Supports Wi-Fi, Zigbee, and Bluetooth. Powerable via live/neutral wiring or USB-C. Manages lighting, scenes, security, and automation from a central touchscreen.

Key Specifications
- Display: 10-inch touchscreen
- Wireless: Wi-Fi + Zigbee + Bluetooth
- Capacity: 128+ Zigbee + 128+ Bluetooth
- Camera: HD ~5MP (intercom + monitoring)
- Features: Doorbell intercom, proximity sensor
- Power: Live/neutral or USB-C
- Installation: Wall-mounted

⚠ Does not switch electrical loads. Central control interface and multi-protocol gateway only.

specs.summary: Input: 100–240V AC, 50/60Hz | Wireless Protocol: Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Zigbee | Display: 1920 × 1200 FHD, 10.1-inch touch screen | Operating Temperature: 0°C to 40°C | Working Humidity: 5%–90% RH (non-condensing) | Size (without base): 259 × 161 × 13 mm | Size (with base): 259 × 161 × 37 mm

limitations: Does not switch electrical loads. Central control interface and multi-protocol gateway only.
dependencies: Wi-Fi network. Live/neutral wiring or USB-C power.
